title: Simple Math
content: What is 2+2?
explanation: Basic arithmetic

title: True or False
content: The Earth is flat.
explanation: The Earth is an oblate spheroid

title: Basic Science
content: What is the chemical symbol for water?
explanation: Water consists of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom

title: Planets
content: Name the planets in our solar system.
answer: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune
explanation: There are 8 planets in our solar system

title: World War II
content: In which year did World War II end?
explanation: World War II ended in 1945 with the surrender of Japan

title: Ancient Civilizations
content: The Roman Empire fell in 476 AD.
explanation: The Western Roman Empire fell in 476 AD

title: Shakespeare
content: Who wrote Romeo and Juliet?
explanation: Romeo and Juliet is one of Shakespeare's most famous tragedies

title: Literary Terms
content: Name three types of literary devices.
explanation: These are common figures of speech used in literature

title: Algebra
content: Solve for x: 2x + 3 = 7
explanation: Subtract 3 from both sides: 2x = 4, then divide by 2: x = 2

title: Geometry
content: What is the sum of angles in a triangle?
explanation: The sum of interior angles in any triangle is 180 degrees

title: Capitals
content: What is the capital of France?
explanation: Paris is the capital and most populous city of France

title: Continents
content: Name the seven continents.
answer: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, South America, Oceania
explanation: There are seven continents on Earth

title: Human Body
content: How many chambers does the human heart have?
explanation: The human heart has four chambers: two atria and two ventricles

title: Photosynthesis
content: Plants use sunlight to make food through photosynthesis.
explanation: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants convert light energy into chemical energy

title: Newton's Laws
content: What is Newton's First Law of Motion?
answer: An object at rest stays at rest, and an object in motion stays in motion with the same speed and in the same direction unless acted upon by an unbalanced force
explanation: This is also known as the Law of Inertia

title: Energy
content: Energy can be created or destroyed.
explanation: According to the Law of Conservation of Energy, energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transformed

title: Elements
content: What element has the atomic number 1?
explanation: Hydrogen is the lightest and most abundant element in the universe

title: Periodic Table
content: Name the three states of matter.
explanation: These are the three fundamental states of matter

title: Programming
content: What does HTML stand for?
explanation: HTML is the standard markup language for creating web pages

title: Algorithms
content: What is the time complexity of binary search?
explanation: Binary search divides the search space in half with each comparison
